content_of_post_to_answer: Why does fear stop so many people?
answer: Fear often stops people not because the risk is too high, but because the mind treats uncertainty as danger. Many people are not afraid of failing; they are afraid of what failure might mean about them. The hidden trap is waiting to feel fearless before acting. Confidence usually comes after repeated contact with the thing we fear, not before it. Fear shrinks when reality replaces imagination.
